← all tutorials

Crash vs Byzantine Faults — interactive CRASH_VS_BYZANTINE.md crash_vs_byzantine.py

A node can fail two ways: crash (it goes silent, never lies) or Byzantine (it stays in the room and lies). Crashes only need a majority — 2f+1 nodes. Lies need a supermajority — 3f+1. Slide N and flip the model to see why one extra degree of badness costs f extra replicas. Every number is recomputed live in JS from the identical formulas as crash_vs_byzantine.py.

① The cluster dial: N nodes, worst-case f faults, the quorum it forces

What to do: pick N and the failure model. The first f nodes are marked faulty (worst case the system is designed to survive). Watch how the surviving quorum and the safety verdict change. Crash needs 2f+1; Byzantine needs 3f+1.
alive / honest crashed (X) Byzantine liar (!)
In words: the quorum is the smallest set that may legally decide. For crashes it's a majority floor(N/2)+1 (two majorities always share ≥1 node, and a crashed node can't lie, so 1 witness is enough). For Byzantine it's a >2/3 supermajority ceil((2N+1)/3), because the shared witness might be a traitor — the overlap must be big enough to hide f liars and still leave ≥1 honest node.

② Quorum intersection — two worst-case quorums, and what survives in the overlap

What this proves: safety lives in the overlap. We shift a second quorum as far right as possible to minimize the overlap, then check whether ≥1 honest node survives there. Crash: overlap ≥1 (and crashes never lie). Byzantine: overlap must be ≥f+1 so that after hiding f traitors ≥1 honest remains.
in quorum S1 in quorum S2 overlap (S1 ∩ S2) outside both
The overlap floor is inclusion-exclusion: |S1 ∩ S2| ≥ 2·Q − N. For Byzantine at the minimal N = 3f+1 this floor is exactly f+1; subtract the f traitors that could hide there and ≥1 honest witness remains — that is the whole reason 3f+1 exists.

③ CFT vs BFT, the worked gold point [f=2], recomputed live in JS

What this proves: these are not made up — the page re-runs the threshold math in your browser on the same formulas as crash_vs_byzantine.py Section E + GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms the JS numbers match the .py exactly.
The headline: tolerating lies instead of just silence costs exactly f extra replicas — 3f+1 − (2f+1) = f. That is the price of adversarial behaviour.
check: … JS formula recompute matches crash_vs_byzantine.py GOLD CHECK (byz_quorum(7)=5, crash_quorum(7)=4, byz_min_n(2)=7, crash_min_n(2)=5, cost_of_lies(2)=2).
